---
title: "X fails to load nVidia driver"
date: 2006-06-03
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by Pixel on 2006-06-03
I'm trying to setup X so that it uses my nvidia driver instead the default "nv" so that I can use xgl and other graphic programs, however, whenever I try to load X with the right driver "nvidia" I get a blank screen and then my monitor goes into standby until I kill X.  Everything is setup like it should be, but heres my xorg.conf just incase im missing something.

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	#Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"RenderAccel"	"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Samsung SyncMaster 215TW"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-81
	VertRefresh	56-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
	Monitor		"Samsung SyncMaster 215TW"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1680x1050"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
          Option  "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection

```

---

### Post by meng on 2006-06-03
Have you tried (while the display is working on driver nv) using synaptic or apt-get to install nvidia-glx? When I did this it automatically fixed everything.

---

### Post by Pixel on 2006-06-03
[QUOTE=meng]Have you tried (while the display is working on driver nv) using synaptic or apt-get to install nvidia-glx? When I did this it automatically fixed everything.[/QUOTE]

I've tried reinstalling the nvidia-glx package using synaptic but it still doesn't work

I think it's a problem with the horizontal and vertical refresh rates in my config, maybe it's not using the right refresh rate or something (it should be 60 at 1680x1050), but i'm not too sure whats wrong, i took those numbers directly from the manufacturers website (Samsung).

Also, the card is a nVidia 7800GS.
